user.title: Tuya BLE Mesh Device
user.description: Enter the BLE MAC address and device type. Default mesh credentials (out_of_mesh/123456) are Tuya factory defaults.

sig_bridge.title: SIG Mesh Bridge Configuration
sig_bridge.description:
Configure the BLE bridge daemon for **{name}**.

The bridge daemon must be running and reachable from Home Assistant. The connection will be tested before continuing.

data_description.bridge_host: IP address or hostname of the Raspberry Pi running the bridge daemon

telink_bridge.title: Telink Bridge Light Configuration
telink_bridge.description:
Configure the BLE bridge daemon for **{name}**.

The bridge daemon must be running and reachable from Home Assistant. The connection will be tested before continuing.

data_description.bridge_host: IP address or hostname of the Raspberry Pi running the bridge daemon

sig_plug.title: SIG Mesh Plug — Auto Provisioning
sig_plug.description:
The device **{name}** will be provisioned automatically.

A random NetKey and AppKey are generated. The DevKey is derived from the ECDH provisioning exchange. All keys are saved securely in the Home Assistant config database.

**Before clicking Submit:**
- Ensure the device is in factory-reset state (LED flashing)
- Ensure it is within BLE range of a local adapter or ESPHome BLE proxy
- Provisioning takes approximately 10 seconds

confirm.title: {name} ({mac})

reauth_confirm.title: Re-enter Credentials
reauth_confirm.description: Connection failed — credentials may have changed. Re-enter to reconnect.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Connection
reconfigure.description: Update the connection settings for this device. The MAC address and device type cannot be changed here.

error.invalid_mac: Invalid MAC address format. Expected XX:XX:XX:XX:XX:XX (colon-separated hex).
error.invalid_bridge_host: Invalid bridge host. Enter a hostname or IP address (not a URL). Loopback and link-local addresses are not allowed.
error.invalid_credential_length: Mesh name and password must each be 16 bytes or fewer.
error.invalid_vendor_id: Invalid Vendor ID. Expected 1–4 hex digits with optional 0x prefix (e.g. 0x1001).
error.bridge_unreachable: Cannot reach bridge daemon at the given host/port. Check that the daemon is running and that Home Assistant can access it on your network.
error.auth_or_mesh_mismatch: Bridge reached but device rejected the mesh credentials. Verify mesh name and password.
error.unsupported_vendor: Vendor ID not recognized. Check BLE snoop logs for the correct vendor bytes.
error.device_not_found: Device MAC not visible from bridge. Ensure the device is powered and within BLE range.
error.timeout: Operation timed out. The bridge or device did not respond in time.
error.unknown_protocol: Protocol negotiation failed. This may indicate a bridge firmware mismatch.
error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to bridge daemon. Verify host/port and that the daemon is running.
error.provisioning_failed: Provisioning failed. Ensure the device is in factory-reset state (LED flashing), within BLE range, and try again. Check HA logs for details.

init.title: Tuya BLE Mesh Options
init.description: Update bridge connection or mesh credentials. Enable **Show advanced settings** for timeout and reconnect options.

advanced.title: Advanced Settings
advanced.description: Adjust debug verbosity, command timeouts, and reconnect storm protection.

data_description.debug_level: Controls verbosity of BLE mesh logs. Warning is recommended for normal use.
data_description.command_timeout: How long to wait for a device response before retrying (3–60 seconds).
data_description.max_reconnects: Limit total reconnect attempts. 0 = keep trying indefinitely.
data_description.reconnect_storm_threshold: Create a repair issue if this many reconnects happen within 5 minutes.

error.invalid_bridge_host: Invalid bridge host. Enter a hostname or IP address (not a URL).
error.bridge_unreachable: Cannot reach bridge daemon. Verify host/port and that the daemon is running.
error.invalid_credential_length: Mesh name and password must each be 16 bytes or fewer.
error.invalid_vendor_id: Invalid Vendor ID. Expected 1–4 hex digits (e.g. 0x1001).

identify.name: Identify device
identify.description: Flash the device LED to make it easy to find.

set_log_level.name: Set log level
set_log_level.description: Change BLE mesh logging verbosity without restarting Home Assistant.

level.name: Level
level.description: Logging level: debug, info, warning, or error.

get_diagnostics.name: Get diagnostics
get_diagnostics.description: Log diagnostic information for a device to the HA log.

device_id.name: Device
device_id.description: The device to get diagnostics for.

confirm.title: Bridge unreachable: {host}:{port}
confirm.description:
The BLE bridge daemon at **{host}:{port}** is not responding.

**Checks:**
1. Is the bridge host powered on?
2. Is the daemon running? Run: `systemctl status tuya-ble-bridge`
3. Is port {port} accessible from Home Assistant?
4. Test: `curl http://{host}:{port}/health`

This issue clears automatically when the daemon comes back online.

reauth_hint.title: Fix: Update mesh credentials
reauth_hint.description:
The device **{device}** is rejecting the stored mesh credentials.

**To fix:**
1. Go to **Settings → Integrations → {device} → Configure**
2. Update mesh name and password to match the device
3. Factory defaults: name=`out_of_mesh`, password=`123456`

If the device was factory-reset, delete and re-add the integration.

confirm.title: Fix: Update vendor ID
confirm.description:
The vendor ID **{vendor_id}** for **{device}** is not recognized.

**To find the correct ID:**
1. Use a BLE sniffer to capture advertisements from the device
2. Look at manufacturer-specific data — vendor bytes are at payload offset `[3:5]`
3. Known IDs: Malmbergs=`0x1001`, AwoX=`0x0160`, Dimond=`0x0211`

**To fix:** Go to **Settings → Integrations → {device} → Configure** and update the Vendor ID.

storm_confirm.title: Acknowledge reconnect storm
storm_confirm.description:
**{device}** reconnected **{count}** times in {window} minutes.

**Checks:**
1. Is the device stable (not rebooting repeatedly)?
2. Is BLE signal adequate (RSSI > −80 dBm)?
3. Check bridge logs for error patterns.

Acknowledging this issue will not stop reconnects — it just clears the notification. Address the underlying cause first.

confirm.title: Fix provisioning failure
confirm.description:
SIG Mesh provisioning failed for **{device}**.

**To fix:**
1. Delete this integration entry
2. Factory-reset the device: 5× rapid power cycle (LED flashes rapidly when ready)
3. Re-add the integration while the LED is flashing

Ensure the device is within BLE range of a local adapter or ESPHome BLE proxy during provisioning.
